description: Questo sub-concetto cattura la velocità della reazione al contatto con la superficie, che può essere immediata (polo positivo) o ritardata (polo negativo).
positive: My fingertip registered the coolness instantly, a fleeting touch, *subito*.
The impact felt immediate, *une sensation instantanée* against my palm.
A rapid response, *sofortig*, as the fabric brushed my cheek.
The water’s touch was instantly perceived, *un contacto inmediato* on my forearm.
My toe felt the floor *instantaneamente*, a quick awareness of the texture.
The metal was cold, the sensation arriving *tout de suite* on my wrist.
The surface yielded quickly, a *velox responsio* to my hand’s pressure.
I felt the chill *instant* on my shoulder, a brief, sharp awareness.
The glass felt cool, the response *prompta* against my fingertip.
The texture registered *instantaneamente*, a quick pulse on my skin.
A rapid response, *schnell*, as I brushed the stone with my elbow.
The coldness was immediate, *un instante* felt on the back of my hand.
My skin reacted *instantly* to the coolness of the tile floor.
A brief, immediate sensation, *in momento*, against my calf.
The surface registered *rapidissime* under my foot, a fleeting chill.
The coldness was *instantaneamente* perceived by my fingertips.
A *prompt* awareness of the coolness on my temple.
*Subito*, I felt the chill against my knuckles.
The coldness struck *instantly*, a quick shiver on my neck.
My heel registered the coolness *in uno sguardo*, a rapid touch.
negative: A slight delay, *un moment* before I felt the coolness on my skin.
There was a noticeable lag, *eine Verzögerung*, before the sensation registered.
The chill arrived *lentamente* against my cheek, a delayed perception.
A brief hesitation, *una demora*, before the coolness was felt on my palm.
I felt it *un poco tarde* against my forearm, a delayed response.
The touch was not immediate; a *lenta percepción* on my toe.
A delayed reaction, *una reazione ritardata*, as the fabric brushed my elbow.
The coolness took a moment, *un retraso*, before I felt it on my wrist.
A slight pause, *una pausa momentánea*, before the sensation registered on my hand.
The chill arrived *lentamente* on my skin, a delayed awareness.
A *långsam* reaction as I touched the stone with my calf.
The coldness was *lentamente* perceived on the back of my hand.
A noticeable hesitation, *una demora perceptible*, before feeling the chill.
There was a slight delay, *un retardo*, before the sensation on my fingertip.
The reaction felt *lento* as I touched the tile floor with my heel.
A brief pause, *una demora breve*, before feeling the coolness on my shoulder.
It took a *poco tempo* for the coldness to register on my temple.
A delayed response, *una respuesta tardía*, against my knuckles.
The chill arrived *lentamente* against my neck, a noticeable lag.
A *lenta sensación* before feeling the coolness on my foot.
